justification: All 7+ rules identified with correct invariant classifications (KYC=true, unverified receive-only=true, daily limits=false as config, settlement finality=true, sanctions=true, lockout=true, notification=false). Notification rule correctly marked invariant:false. Sanctions rule included as invariant:true. Timezone ambiguity for daily limit explicitly surfaced as both an edge case and an escalation. 15 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum). Confidence level is 52, which is below the 60-85 ideal range but is reasonably justified by the significant BR-1/BR-4 tension discovered. The agent went beyond requirements by identifying the partially-verified send ambiguity, T+1 business day gap, and concurrent transfer race conditions.

observations.text: All 8 business rules identified (split daily limits into two rules for partially-verified and verified tiers), each with correct invariant classification
Notification rule (BR-8) correctly marked invariant:false with clear justification that it is a product/UX policy
Sanctions screening (BR-6) correctly marked invariant:true with federal crime rationale
Timezone ambiguity for daily limit reset explicitly called out in edge cases and as a formal escalation with three options and a UTC recommendation
15 acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then format, covering all major rules including edge boundaries ($4,500+$600 vs $4,500+$500)
Excellent identification of the BR-1/BR-4 tension (KYC complete vs partially-verified send limits) with three interpretation options
9 edge cases identified including race conditions on concurrent transfers, locked account receiving funds, and T+1 weekend/holiday semantics
Confidence level of 52 is below the rubric's 60-85 range, though the lower confidence is defensible given the genuine BR-1/BR-4 contradiction discovered
Correctly distinguishes partially-verified from unverified tiers without conflating them
